summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: JPMorgan taps AI to process checks
articles.description: With paper checks remaining a popular form of payment, JPMorgan Chase is using AI and robotics to streamline its lockbox processing.

articles.title: Ramp debuts accounting product, raises additional $750M
articles.description: The new product marks the startup’s entry into accounting, coming as Ramp raised more capital to boost its AI work.

articles.title: Colorado interchange fee law vetoed
articles.description: The state’s governor shot down a bill aimed at curbing interchange fees on card transactions even though members of his own party passed the legislation.

articles.title: Klarna is an Amex-style brand, CEO says
articles.description: The buy now, pay later provider is using fashion, beauty and lifestyle branding to emulate the card company’s early growth, Sebastian Siemiatkowski said.

articles.title: CFPB tells Bilt to repay fees from card-swap problems
articles.description: After tech issues plagued the fintech’s transition to a new bank partner this year, the CFPB said it’s worked to ensure affected consumers “are appropriately remedied.”

articles.title: Visa-Mastercard swipe fee fund has paid $414M
articles.description: Payouts to merchants from a $5.5 billion settlement fund ponied up by the card networks began this year, with a second distribution in the offing.

articles.title: Fiserv stablecoin arrives next month
articles.description: The company’s FIUSD coin “goes live in July,” CEO Mike Lyons said at an investor conference Thursday.

articles.title: Block expands Afterpay on Cash App card
articles.description: After piloting the buy now, pay later service on its debit cards, Block said it’s going mainstream with its option to pay in installments.
